2603.26244.title: Automating Domain-Driven Design: Experience with a Prompting Framework
2603.26244.paper_summary: Domain-driven design (DDD) is a powerful design technique for architecting complex software systems. This paper introduces a prompting framework that automates core DDD activities through structured large language model (LLM) interactions. We decompose DDD into five sequential steps: (1) establishing an ubiquitous language, (2) simulating event storming, (3) identifying bounded contexts, (4) designing aggregates, and (5) mapping to technical architecture. In a case study, we validated the prompting framework against real-world requirements from FTAPI's enterprise platform. While the first steps consistently generate valuable and usable artifacts, later steps show how minor errors or inaccuracies can propagate and accumulate. Overall, the framework excels as a collaborative sparring partner for building actionable documentation, such as glossaries and context maps, but not for full automation. This allows the experts to concentrate their discussion on the critical trade-offs. In our evaluation, Steps 1 to 3 worked well, but the accumulated errors rendered the artifacts generated from Steps 4 and 5 impractical. Our findings show that LLMs can enhance, but not replace, architectural expertise, offering a practical tool to reduce the effort and overhead of DDD while preserving human-centric decision-making.
2603.26244.paper_summary_zh: 領域驅動設計（DDD）是一種強大的設計技術，用於架構複雜的軟體系統。<lb/>本文介紹了一個提示框架，通過結構化的大型語言模型（LLM）互動自動化核心DDD活動。<lb/>我們將DDD分解為五個連續步驟：（1）建立普遍語言，（2）模擬事件風暴，（3）識別邊界上下文，（4）設計聚合，以及（5）映射到技術架構。<lb/>在一個案例研究中，我們根據FTAPI企業平台的實際需求驗證了該提示框架。<lb/>雖然前幾個步驟始終生成有價值且可用的工件，但後面的步驟顯示出小錯誤或不準確性如何傳播和積累。<lb/>總體而言，該框架作為建立可操作文檔（如詞彙表和上下文圖）的協作對手表現出色，但並不適合完全自動化。<lb/>這使得專家能夠將討論集中在關鍵的取捨上。<lb/>在我們的評估中，步驟1到3運作良好，但積累的錯誤使得從步驟4和5生成的工件變得不切實際。<lb/>我們的研究結果顯示，LLM可以增強但不能取代架構專業知識，提供一種實用工具以減少DDD的努力和開銷，同時保留以人為中心的決策。
2603.26244.author: Tobias Eisenreich et.al.
2603.26244.authors: Tobias Eisenreich, Husein Jusic, Stefan Wagner
